steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text describes a carving that changes over time: starting with an “irregular and misty outline” and developing “distincter grace and beauty.” The word replacing “assumed” must describe how an inanimate object comes to possess a new quality through a process.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Acquired: Means came to have or took on — fits perfectly because the carving gradually gained precision as Drowne worked on it. ✓
  • Choice B — Acknowledged: Means recognized or admitted — an inanimate wooden figure cannot consciously recognize anything. ✗
  • Choice C — Imitated: Means copied or mimicked something else, but the text says the figure became more precise through carving, not that it mimicked precision from another source. ✗
  • Choice D — Speculated: Means guessed or theorized — an inanimate object cannot speculate, and the context is about gaining form, not forming a hypothesis. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
